how_profiles_are_read: The current matcher reads candidate_profiles from MongoDB collection talent_intelligence.candidate_profiles through recommender.py and faiss_indexer.py. FAISS id_map gives profile_id/candidate_id rows, then recommender fetches MongoDB documents by profile_id with status=success.

root_causes.finding: Grounded profiles were imported, but some documents still contain weak bio.full_name values inherited from OCR or noisy headers.
The previous text builder injected raw full_name and summary into the embedding text, which amplified OCR titles and placeholders like O Ariana, Tunisia or RESUME OBJECTIVE.
The previous scorer used reliability_score/profile_kind lightly, but it ignored hallucination_risk, fields_nullified and weak display-name handling.
The previous recommender displayed any non-empty full_name, even when it looked like a title or OCR header.
FAISS itself is not the root cause; it indexed whatever candidate text was built upstream. The issue came from the text and quality signals fed into it.

why_false_names_appeared: False names appeared because noisy full_name values were imported from grounded profiles, then copied both into embedding text and into report display logic without suspicion checks.
conclusion: The issue was a mix of noisy source content plus matching-layer consumption problems. MongoDB contained some weak names, but the larger failure was that matching treated those weak names as semantic evidence and trusted labels.
